mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and a deceleration in CPI inflation indicates robust consumer demand without pricing pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production directly boosts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during monetary easing cycles.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically insignificant during periods of rapid interest rate hikes, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of expansionary INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage income, which mechanically supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns with Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns are negative in >60% of months with payroll increases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sudden contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive forward returns in >50% of triggered instances, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance during periods of active government housing stimulus programs.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and simultaneously increasing unemployment creates stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns when quarterly GDP growth remains above 2%, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2% confirms sustained economic expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during periods of low inflation.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power or reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4% annually, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of high-GDP-growth quarters.
